sentence: Las casas están cerradas por el ciclón que viene.
ipa: lah kasah estaŋ seraðah poɾ el sikloŋ ke bjene
gloss_en: The houses are shut because of the coming hurricane.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Mi asere comió arroz con frijoles en el solar.
ipa: mi aseɾe komio aros koŋ fɾixoleh eŋ el solaɾ
gloss_en: My buddy ate rice with beans in the tenement yard.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: El niño pequeño juega en el parque los domingos.
ipa: el niɲo pekeɲo xweɣa eŋ el paɾke loh dominɡoh
gloss_en: The little boy plays in the park on Sundays.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per penny2002.

sentence: ¿Vamos a coger la guagua para ir al centro?
ipa: bamoh a koxeɾ la ɡaɡa paɾa iɾ al sentɾo
gloss_en: Shall we take the bus downtown?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per hualde2005.

sentence: Ese muchacho es muy bueno tocando el tambor.
ipa: ese mutʃatʃo eh mui bweno tokando el tamboɾ
gloss_en: That boy is really good at playing the drum.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: La luz del faro brilla sobre el malecón de noche.
ipa: la lus del faɾo bɾiʝa sobɾe el malekoŋ de notʃe
gloss_en: The lighthouse beam shines over the seafront at night.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per penny2002.

sentence: Compré diez mangos maduros en la placita.
ipa: kompɾe djes manɡoh maðuɾoh eŋ la plasita
gloss_en: I bought ten ripe mangoes at the little market.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: El corazón de la ciudad late con son y rumba.
ipa: el koɾasoŋ de la sjuða late koŋ soŋ i rumba
gloss_en: The heart of the city beats with son and rumba.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: Los pescadores salen al amanecer con sus redes.
ipa: loh peskaðoɾeh saleŋ al amaneseɾ koŋ suh reðeh
gloss_en: The fishermen set out at dawn with their nets.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per penny2002.

sentence: ¿Cuántas personas vienen a la fiesta esta noche?
ipa: kuantah peɾsonah bjeneŋ a la fjesta esta notʃe
gloss_en: How many people are coming to the party tonight?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: La región oriental guarda muchos pueblos antiguos.
ipa: la rexioŋ oɾjental ɡaɾda mutʃoh pwebloh antiɡoh
gloss_en: The eastern region holds many old villages.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: El perro corre detrás del carro por la avenida.
ipa: el pero kore detɾah del karo poɾ la abeniða
gloss_en: The dog runs after the car along the avenue.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); alveolar trill [r] preserved per penny2002.

sentence: No quiero que llueva sobre los cañaverales hoy.
ipa: no kjeɾo ke ʝweba sobɾe loh kaɲabeɾaleh oi
gloss_en: I don't want it to rain over the canefields today.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Mi hermano vive lejos, cerca del río grande.
ipa: mi eɾmano bibe lexoh seɾka del rio ɡɾande
gloss_en: My brother lives far away, near the big river.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: La mujer canta una canción vieja y hermosa.
ipa: la muxeɾ kanta una kansioŋ bjexa i eɾmosa
gloss_en: The woman sings an old and beautiful song.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per penny2002.

sentence: Dame un vaso de agua bien fría, por favor.
ipa: dame uŋ baso de aɡa bjeŋ fɾia poɾ faboɾ
gloss_en: Give me a glass of very cold water, please.
notes: word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ]; Romance rising diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je/we] per hualde2005.

sentence: Aquellos árboles dan sombra en el batey.
ipa: akeʝoh aɾboleh daŋ sombɾa eŋ el batei
gloss_en: Those trees give shade in the batey.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.

sentence: El señor mayor cuenta historias de su juventud.
ipa: el seɲoɾ maʝoɾ kwenta istoɾjah de su xubentu
gloss_en: The old gentleman tells stories of his youth.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); yeísmo: ⟨ll/y⟩ realised [ʝ] per penny2002.

sentence: Las estrellas alumbran el campo durante la madrugada.
ipa: lah estɾeʝah alumbɾaŋ el kampo duɾante la madɾuɣaða
gloss_en: The stars light up the countryside in the early morning.
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); word-final /n/ velarisation to [ŋ] per hualde2005.

sentence: Buenos días, ¿cómo amaneció usted por acá?
ipa: bwenoh diah komo amanesio uste poɾ aka
gloss_en: Good morning, how did you wake up around here?
notes: coda /s/ aspiration to [h] (Caribbean weakening); seseo: ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s] per fontanella_de_weinberg1992.
